title: The Banker

jokes.text: Too loud. Too too loud, too loud. But effective. Look.
jokes.setup: Jim tries to get Michael to lower his voice during party announcement
jokes.punchline: Michael admits it's too loud but defends it as 'effective'
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - acknowledges criticism while missing the point entirely. Strong character integration and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Michael, you need to lower your voice. Everyone can hear you.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What? No, no, no. This is perfect. People need to hear the announcement.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: But you're basically yelling. Can't you just use a normal voice?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, fine, fine. Yes, it's loud. But you know what? It's effective. People are listening, aren't they?

jokes.text: My diabolical plot is on hold for christmas.
jokes.setup: Christmas party announcement context
jokes.punchline: Dwight casually mentions having a diabolical plot on pause
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight line - unexpectedly dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. High character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have a diabolical plot, but I've decided to put it on pause for the holidays.

jokes.text: My heart just melts with the sound of children singing. [snickers] Not really. I'm just tired. The days are short. I don't know. Maybe I'm depressed.
jokes.setup: Angela seemingly expresses Christmas sentiment about children singing
jokes.punchline: Immediate reversal revealing her actual cynical state
jokes.explanation: Classic Angela misdirection - sets up false sentiment then undercuts it with brutal honesty.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: There is nothing more beautiful than the sound of children singing in unison. It's like angels.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: Of course, these children are all going to Hell.

jokes.text: Hey, rockefeller center. I've actually been to rock center, And this is nothing like that.
jokes.setup: Jim sarcastically compares their office tree to Rockefeller Center
jokes.punchline: Pam deflates the comparison with understated reality check
jokes.explanation: Solid observational humor but fairly predictable. Good economy but not special.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Our office tree is basically the Rockefeller Center tree.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's a small potted plant that Kevin knocked over twice.

jokes.text: Why don't we talk more about it instead of doing it?
jokes.setup: Discussion about office camaraderie and warm feelings
jokes.punchline: Stanley's cynical suggestion to avoid actual participation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley - cutting through corporate nonsense with lazy cynicism. High character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You know what the secret to office camaraderie is? Don't actually participate. Just smile and nod while everyone else wastes their time with trust falls and team building exercises.

jokes.text: Why would you start so high? 27... 3, 2, 1... 26...
jokes.setup: Countdown to tree unveiling starting at 30
jokes.punchline: Jim questions the logic while Pam does proper countdown overlapping with original
jokes.explanation: Good observational comedy about arbitrary countdown logic, enhanced by overlapping execution.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Wait, why are we counting down from 30? It's like 20 feet away.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's tradition, Jim.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: A tree doesn't need a countdown. It's not a bomb.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Just count with me. 30... 29... 28...

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: This is ridiculous. 27... 26...

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You're doing it wrong. Start over. 30... 29...

jokes.text: You didn't decorate it. Why would you wrap it in a sheet If you're not covering anything up? Is--is it fake?
jokes.setup: Tree unveiling reveals undecorated artificial tree
jokes.punchline: Logical questions expose the absurdity of the whole unveiling
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of logical questions that expose corporate absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So we're unveiling a tree that's... not decorated?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's supposed to be a surprise.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: The surprise is that there's nothing there?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: It's artificial.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Right, but why unveil it before decorating it? Isn't the decorated tree the thing worth unveiling?

jokes.text: And we're supposed to applaud you For you taking a giant diaper off a fake tree?
jokes.setup: Underwhelming tree unveiling ceremony
jokes.punchline: Jim's brutal but accurate description of what just happened
jokes.explanation: 'Giant diaper' is a perfectly absurd visual description that deflates corporate pomp. High quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That was the most pathetic Christmas tree I've ever seen. It looked like it died in October and we just didn't notice.

jokes.text: My cat killed a turtledove. The french hens have started pulling out my hair To make a nest.
jokes.setup: Erin's secret santa is giving her the twelve days of Christmas
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd consequences of receiving live animals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation of what would actually happen with live Christmas gift animals.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Oh my God, a partridge in a pear tree! This is the best Secret Santa ever!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Wait, two turtle doves? Where am I supposed to keep two turtle doves?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Three French hens?! My apartment smells like a petting zoo!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Four calling birds — they won't stop calling! I think they're summoning something!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: FIVE GOLDEN RINGS! Oh thank God, finally something that's not alive. Wait, why is there a live goose attached to this ring?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Six geese a-laying... they're laying everywhere, Erin! There's goose poop in my hair!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Seven swans a-swimming — I don't even have a pool! They're in my bathtub! They're in my toilet!

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: My landlord just evicted me. Eight maids a-milking showed up but there's nothing to milk and they're very confused and also angry!

jokes.text: Is it my fault that the first eight days There's basically 30 birds?
jokes.setup: Andy reveals he's giving Erin the twelve days of Christmas
jokes.punchline: His oblivious defense of sending 30 birds to someone's apartment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - mathematically correct but completely missing the bigger picture. Good character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm giving Erin the twelve days of Christmas.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Thirty birds? You're sending thirty birds to her apartment?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's a Christmas carol, Dwight. That's what you do.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Andy, those birds are going to destroy her place.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: She loves birds! She's going to love this.

jokes.text: I slipped a note to jim 11 weeks ago, And he said I could do it. [giggles] [tearfully] oh...It's been a long journey... But...[sniffles] I'm santa claus!
jokes.setup: Phyllis explains how she got to be Santa
jokes.punchline: Overly emotional reaction to getting to play Santa at office party
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy - Phyllis treating office Santa role like major life achievement.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Well, I got to be Santa because Bob Vance, Vance Refrigeration, thought I had the right temperament for it.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: And honestly, it's been such an honor. I've never felt more alive, more purposeful. This is what I was meant to do. *tears up* I'm sorry, I'm just... this means everything to me.

jokes.text: What if you've been really, really bad? More 'evil' than strictly 'wrong.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of what bad children get from Santa
jokes.punchline: Creed's specific distinction between 'wrong' and 'evil'
jokes.explanation: Classic Creed - casually revealing he's done things beyond 'wrong' and into 'evil' territory. Perfect character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Santa doesn't bring presents to bad children. He brings them coal.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What about evil children?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Presents.

jokes.text: I had the exact same idea For catching osama bin laden. I would simply send him a different piece each day. He would assemble it To find himself... In jail!
jokes.setup: Dwight suspects his secret santa gift (machine parts) is a trap
jokes.punchline: His absurd plan to catch Bin Laden with the same method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic - paranoid but internally consistent. The Bin Laden twist is unexpectedly absurd and perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: If this is a trap, I will find out. And when I do, I'm going to use this same method to catch Bin Laden.

jokes.text: But really...I just get the recipe from the box. That's funny. Is it funny? I thought it was more... Interesting than funny.
jokes.setup: Kevin shares that his lasagna recipe comes from the box
jokes.punchline: Kevin's confusion about whether his own story is funny
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Kevin not understanding his own comedic timing. Mild but fits character.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: It's from a box.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: ...Of course it is.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: What? Why is everyone laughing?

jokes.text: I think my water just broke. That's too funny. Oscar and the warehouse guy. Go, oscar! Go, gay warehouse guy!
jokes.setup: Someone makes pregnancy joke about water breaking
jokes.punchline: Others cheer on Oscar and warehouse guy as if they're a couple
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor but not particularly sharp. Sets up romantic subplot awkwardly.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Oh my God, her water broke!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: What? No, that's just—

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse Worker
dialogue.line: Come on, buddy, let's go!

dialogue.speaker: Office
dialogue.line: Go, go, go! Come on!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Guys, stop it!

jokes.text: Hey, little girl, what would you like for christmas? Ooh! You have been a very naughty girl, I see.
jokes.setup: Michael appears as competing Santa
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate Santa interaction creating maximum discomfort
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor but relies on inappropriate Santa trope. Creates discomfort but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Ho ho ho! Merry Christmas! I'm Santa!

dialogue.speaker: Child
dialogue.line: Are you the real Santa?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, that depends on how you define 'real.' I mean, Santa is a state of mind, you know? It's about the magic inside your heart.

dialogue.speaker: Parent
dialogue.line: Um, okay... come on, sweetie.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, wait! I have something for you! It's a Dundie Award! For being the cutest kid at the Dunder Mifflin Christmas party!

jokes.text: It's insane! A woman santa? Where does it stop? No!
jokes.setup: Michael learns Phyllis is playing Santa
jokes.punchline: His absurd outrage at gender of fictional character
jokes.explanation: Good Michael absurdity - treating office Santa casting as serious gender issue. Strong character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis is Santa? No, no, no. Santa is a man. Santa has a beard, he wears a suit, he's jolly. Phyllis is a woman. She doesn't have a beard, she wears sensible shoes, and she's never jolly.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I can be jolly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No. No you can't, Phyllis. And frankly, the fact that you're even suggesting that you could be Santa is offensive to the entire North Pole.

jokes.text: I have a bunch of letters cut out of magazines in my desk. You can use those.
jokes.setup: Michael suggests anonymous note to remove Phyllis as Santa
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he has ransom note materials ready
jokes.explanation: Dark humor - Michael casually admitting to having kidnapper supplies ready. Good character detail.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We could write an anonymous note telling her she's not Santa anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I have the materials in my desk. Magazines, scissors, glue.

jokes.text: If this were russia, yeah. Sure, everybody would go to one santa... At which point the kgb would arrest you And send you to siberia. It's a good thing russia doesn't exist anymore.
jokes.setup: Michael's extended metaphor about competing Santas
jokes.punchline: 'Russia doesn't exist anymore' as casual historical fact
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael - overwrought metaphor ending with confident but wrong historical claim. High quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Russia doesn't exist anymore.

jokes.text: Why pay more to sit next to old tranny claus over there When you can sit on my lap? Phyllis is only pretending to be a man. I'm the real thing!
jokes.setup: Michael competing for Santa customers
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate and offensive sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Offensive humor that relies on slurs and inappropriate content. Low craft, dates poorly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, listen up. I'm going to be honest with you — Santa's a fat guy in a red suit who breaks into your house at night. That's literally a home invasion. But here's the thing — I can do it better, faster, and I won't judge your cookie selection. Plus, I'm not on any lists. Well, I'm on lists, but not that kind of list. Most of them.

jokes.text: No, it's not, like, penis-wise.
jokes.setup: Michael's previous inappropriate Santa comments
jokes.punchline: Attempting to clarify but making it worse
jokes.explanation: Classic 'making it worse' comedy but relies on crude humor. Functional but not clever.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, I just want to clarify something about what I said earlier regarding Santa. I was not trying to be inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I was simply making an observation about Santa's physical appearance and what I perceive to be his somewhat suggestive outfit.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Which, when you think about it, is basically just a red suit and a big white beard, so really, who's to say what's appropriate?

jokes.text: Michael, it's me, kevin. Phyllis says I'm too big for her lap.
jokes.setup: Kevin approaches Michael's Santa
jokes.punchline: Sad revelation about being rejected by other Santa
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Kevin's innocent acceptance of Santa rejection. Creates sympathy and discomfort.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I've been coming to this Santa for three years. Last year, I asked him for a real diamond ring. He said, 'Kevin, that's not realistic.' So I said, 'Well, what about a fake diamond ring?' And he said, 'Kevin, you need to set your expectations lower.' This year I asked him for socks. He said, 'Kevin, I can't help you.'

jokes.text: But santa remembers a reindeer That was just a tiny bit different as well.
jokes.setup: Michael comforting Kevin about being rejected
jokes.punchline: Rudolph reference that kind of calls Kevin a freak
jokes.explanation: Michael attempting comfort but accidentally insulting. Good character-driven irony.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hey, you know what? Rudolph was rejected too. And you know what happened to him?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: He found acceptance?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, he was used for his one special quality and then thrown away. But before that, he was happy!

jokes.text: [quietly] oh, my god. [grunts] It's really comfortable. [gasping] [strained] what would you like for christmas, little boy? [panting]
jokes.setup: Kevin sits on Michael's lap
jokes.punchline: Michael being crushed but trying to maintain Santa character
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy - Michael's commitment to Santa role despite being crushed. Visual gag works well.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Ho ho ho... Kevin, you're a little heavy for Santa's lap.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: What? I'm not that big.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, it's fine. Ho ho ho. I can feel my legs again... any second now.

jokes.text: I don't know. I-I didn't know you were gonna ask me that. Well, what did you think was going to happen? I didn't know. Nobody's ever let me sit on their lap before.
jokes.setup: Michael asks Kevin what he wants for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Kevin's sad admission about never sitting on anyone's lap
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking character moment that's both funny and sad. Kevin's innocence makes it work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, what do you want for Christmas?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I don't know. I've never sat on anyone's lap before.

jokes.text: Can you give me some choices? 'cause I really don't wanna mess up on this list. What about if I tell you the things I don't want?
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to think of Christmas wishes
jokes.punchline: His overthinking of what should be simple childhood interaction
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy - Kevin treating Santa list like important business decision. Perfect escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I want a Nintendo Switch.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Actually, no. I want a PlayStation 5.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Wait, what if I want an Xbox? Do I like Xbox? I don't know anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Maybe I should just ask for socks. Everyone likes socks.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: But what if the socks don't fit? What if they're the wrong material?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: This is too stressful. I'm just going to ask for a gift card.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: But then I have to decide what to buy with it. That's even worse.

jokes.text: You get a thousand helium balloons attached to you So santa doesn't have to go through this again.
jokes.setup: Michael frustrated with Kevin's indecision
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific punishment disguised as Christmas gift
jokes.explanation: Dark but creative solution to Kevin problem. Good visual absurdity with practical purpose.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, I'm getting you a Christmas gift. It's going to be a framed photo of you from today, but I'm going to have it printed at a size that's just slightly too big to fit in any standard frame you own. You'll have to special order a custom frame, which will cost more than the photo itself.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: ...What?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Merry Christmas.

jokes.text: Don't make me get bob involved. What would bob do? Never mind. I shouldn't have said that.
jokes.setup: Phyllis threatens to involve her husband Bob
jokes.punchline: Immediate backtrack suggesting Bob might be dangerous
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - implies Bob is mob-connected or violent. Good economy and character building.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I'm going to call my husband Bob.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. Let's not do anything rash here.

jokes.text: [laughs] yeah! [both chuckling] Totally. I admit it. It's me. Seriously? [chuckling] are you serious? Yeah. Totally serious. I'm your secret santa. Busted!
jokes.setup: Erin asks Jim if he's her secret santa
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic admission that Erin takes seriously
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim sarcasm that gets misunderstood. Good character interaction but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Are you my secret santa?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yes, Erin, I am your secret santa.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Oh my god!

jokes.text: Matt's a pretty good-lookin' dude, don't you think? I'll just leave it here with ya.
jokes.setup: Delivery person commenting on Matt's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Immediate retreat when Darryl makes it awkward
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor about workplace attraction. Sets up subplot but not particularly strong.

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse Worker
dialogue.line: Wow, Matt is really attractive.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Yeah, he knows.

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse Worker
dialogue.line: ...I'm gonna go.

jokes.text: You can't yell out, 'I need this, I need this,' As you pin down an employee on your lap.
jokes.setup: Michael's aggressive Santa behavior with Ryan
jokes.punchline: Jim's clinical description of how inappropriate it sounds
jokes.explanation: Great Jim observation - makes Michael realize how creepy he sounds. Good economy and delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So Michael basically tackled Santa, who happened to be Ryan, and when we all just stared at him in confusion, he goes, 'What? It's not real.' Which—I mean, technically accurate, but also deeply inappropriate in every other way.

jokes.text: After you almost killed me? Michael, I had you. I just wanna try phyllis.
jokes.setup: Michael appeals to Kevin after Santa vote
jokes.punchline: Kevin's matter-of-fact assessment and desire for variety
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier physical comedy. Kevin's casual brutality is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, come on. You're a smart guy. You know Santa isn't real.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I know he's not real. I just think it'd be cool if he was.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, but—

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: And I want to see what he'd do with the office.

jokes.text: How do you know it's a gun? What else does it look like? Not a gun. Well, unless the missing pieces is a gun, You don't have a gun. Not a gun.
jokes.setup: Creed thinks his secret santa gift is assembling into a gun
jokes.punchline: Jim's logical but repeated insistence it's 'not a gun'
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan from Jim, enhanced by Creed's paranoia. The repetition of 'not a gun' works well.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: This is a gun. It's definitely a gun.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's not a gun.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Then what is it?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: It's a birdhouse.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: A birdhouse? That's the worst gun I've ever seen.

jokes.text: Let the party begin! I don't ask for much from christmas. But when you need my help because I am ruining everything, Don't look at me!
jokes.setup: Phyllis starts party while Michael sulks
jokes.punchline: Michael's contradiction - claiming he doesn't ask for much while actively ruining party
jokes.explanation: Good Michael self-awareness mixed with complete lack of self-awareness. Classic character contradiction.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't ask for much. I just want people to have fun and enjoy themselves at my parties.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But if anyone leaves early, I will hunt them down and kill them.

jokes.text: Behold... Jesus christ. And I bring to you... Glad christmas tidings.
jokes.setup: Michael's dramatic entrance
jokes.punchline: Awkward Biblical language delivery as office Jesus
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from Santa to Jesus. Michael's awkward biblical speech is in character but not exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I come before you today as your savior. I have risen from the dead... my inbox. And I say unto you, let there be... synergy. Amen.

jokes.text: Oh...The antichrist. You cannot push religion. Oh, but I can push drugs in here. Is that what you're saying? Well, you have to pick one or the other. Your choice. Pick your poison.
jokes.setup: Toby objects to Michael playing Jesus at work
jokes.punchline: Michael's false dichotomy between religion and drugs
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael logic - creating false choice and missing the point entirely. Good character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Michael, you can't play Jesus at work.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh, so what are you saying? Either I play Jesus or I do drugs? Those are my only two options?

jokes.text: All right, sweetie, I'll just tell her it's michael.
jokes.setup: Andy asks Phyllis not to reveal he's Erin's secret santa
jokes.punchline: Phyllis casually agreeing to frame Michael instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Phyllis casually agreeing to lie and implicate Michael. Great economy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Phyllis, please don't tell anyone that I'm Erin's Secret Santa.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Oh, don't worry. I'll tell her it was Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What? No, why would you do that?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I'm just kidding. I won't tell anyone.

jokes.text: Oscar's pate's great. I could eat it all day, every day. How come the good ones are never straight, right?
jokes.setup: Pam trying to set up Oscar and Matt
jokes.punchline: Awkward stereotype comment in front of both men
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Pam's well-intentioned but awkward matchmaking. Relies on stereotype for humor.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You know, Oscar, Matt is also very organized and detail-oriented. You two are so similar!

dialogue.speaker: Matt
dialogue.line: Yeah, we do seem to have a lot in common.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Right? You're both so... you know... meticulous about things. It's great!

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: Pam, are you trying to set us up because we're both Latino?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: What? No! I just thought... I mean, you both like... organized things.

jokes.text: Yes. They're the only two gay guys I know. But they should be together.
jokes.setup: Pam's confession about trying to set up Oscar and Matt
jokes.punchline: Her admission of flawed logic behind matchmaking
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about matchmaking logic. Pam recognizing her flawed reasoning.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I was trying to set up Oscar and Matt because they're both... well, I thought they had things in common. But then I realized I was just matching them because they're both... I don't know, I wasn't really thinking it through. I think I just wanted to feel like I did something nice, even if the logic was completely wrong.

jokes.text: We don't have a north pole branch. Idiot! Oh, yes. It's space garbage. Dwight's gonna be able to build himself a friend.
jokes.setup: Package arrives from 'North Pole branch'
jokes.punchline: Angela's harsh reality check and Kevin's assessment of Dwight's gift
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Kevin's observation about Dwight building a friend. Angela's harshness is in character.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: This is ridiculous. There is no North Pole branch. Dwight made this up.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Yeah, but the chocolate is real.

jokes.text: Yeah, except for cheating on your wife. Adultery's a sin. Look it up in the bible, people. A lotta fire where you're going. Better get used to it. You're going to h-e-l-l, double hockey sticks. Goin' to hell, stanley.
jokes.setup: Michael as Jesus commenting on Stanley being good this year
jokes.punchline: Immediate callout of Stanley's adultery with childish hell euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Michael's casual adultery callout. 'Double hockey sticks' is perfect childish touch.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Stanley, you've been very good this year.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: Thank you, Jesus.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But I know about your affair with Cynthia.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You're going to... the bad place.

jokes.text: That's fantastic! You can make another dress that goes past your feet.
jokes.setup: Someone gets fabric as gift
jokes.punchline: Michael's backhanded comment about their clothing
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor from Michael's inappropriate comment. Good economy but not particularly sharp.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh wow, fabric! You know what? That's actually perfect because I've been meaning to tell you - your wardrobe could really use some... updating. I mean, I'm not saying you dress bad, but if anyone deserves a do-over, it's you. So this is like the universe telling you something, you know?

jokes.text: I will give you a hint. His last name is christ. He has the power of flight. He can heal leopards. I am jesus, david.
jokes.setup: Michael making David guess who he's dressed as
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd Jesus powers ending with leopard healing
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation with 'heal leopards' being perfectly random and specific. High quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, David, guess who I am. I can turn water into wine.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Jesus?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yes! But there's more. I can walk on water.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Still Jesus.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I can raise the dead.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Okay, definitely Jesus.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I can heal lepers.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Michael, that's still—

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I can heal leopards.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not a Jesus power.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I have it.

jokes.text: Because phyllis, a woman, Has 'uslurped' my role as santa.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his Jesus costume to David
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'usurped' while making sexist complaint
jokes.explanation: Good Michael malapropism - 'uslurped' shows his education level while maintaining outrage. Character integration strong.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not just doing this for the attention. Jesus was a real person who did real things.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And if you think about it, Jesus was kind of like a temp worker. He came in, did his job, and then left. Except he came back.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Women have really up-surped the workplace. They've completely up-surped it.

jokes.text: Earlier today, this office needed a santa. And then it needed a second santa. And then it needed a jesus. Now, it needs a michael. And that's one suit phyllis cannot fit into.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about the day's progression
jokes.punchline: Final dig at Phyllis disguised as leadership statement
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - builds logical progression then lands on petty insult. Good Michael character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I've learned a lot today. About leadership. About myself. About what it takes to be a great boss. And I think the key is to really listen to your employees, understand their needs, and help them grow. Unless you're Phyllis. Then you're just sort of... dead weight.

jokes.text: I...Don't want to say. But it's fun to stay at the... Where? Holiday inn. I don't--
jokes.setup: Michael won't say his PIN number but gives clue
jokes.punchline: YMCA reference that Dwight misinterprets as Holiday Inn
jokes.explanation: Clever PIN clue setup with Dwight's literal-minded wrong guess. Good wordplay structure.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, here's a clue. It's the acronym of a song that makes you want to get up and dance.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Holiday Inn?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, Dwight. Y-M-C-A.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Oh! Holiday Inn.

jokes.text: I suppose a gentleman might throw in the towel At this point. Guess what. Not gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Andy's romantic pursuit seemingly failing
jokes.punchline: False surrender immediately contradicted
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment - pretending to be gentlemanly then revealing persistence. Misdirection works.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, I give up. I'm done trying.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So anyway, I was thinking we could grab dinner this Friday?

jokes.text: Ryan, you are my son, And... Pam, you're my wife, and jim... And angela and phyllis, you are my...Grandmas. And stanley, you're...Our mailman.
jokes.setup: Michael describing office as family
jokes.punchline: Increasingly awkward family assignments, especially Stanley as mailman
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation with Stanley as mailman being perfect non-family role. Strong Michael character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, we're like a family here. A real family.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And like any family, we all have our roles. Stanley, you're the mailman.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: I am the mailman.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yes. And Pam, you're the... the mother figure.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: I don't want to be the mother figure.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Too late. And Jim, you're the... the disappointing son who never calls.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And Dwight, you're the weird uncle that everyone avoids at Thanksgiving.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I am honored.

jokes.text: I call the school. I tell them I'm the pediatrician. They patch me through to his secretary. I use my little girl voice. Badda-bing, badda-boom.
jokes.setup: Creed explaining how he gets through to David Wallace
jokes.punchline: Elaborate scheme involving impersonation and child voice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed - casually reveals elaborate criminal-adjacent scheme. 'Little girl voice' detail makes it exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: I have David Wallace's ear. I call him up, I do a little child voice, I say 'Daddy, I'm scared.' And he comes running.

jokes.text: It's not sweetie. It's michael scott. What the hell-- Michael--[huffs] I have never-- never, ever--
jokes.setup: David realizes he's been tricked by Creed's scheme
jokes.punchline: His outrage at being deceived by office employees
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Creed's scheme. David's frustration is earned and relatable.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You know what? I've been coming to this office for years, and I thought I knew these people. I thought we had trust. But Creed? Creed just made me look like a fool in front of the entire company.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: David, come on. It's just a little office prank. We've all been there.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: A little prank? Michael, the man convinced me that the vending machine was sentient! I spent twenty minutes apologizing to a machine! To a MACHINE! These people have no respect, and frankly, neither do I anymore.

jokes.text: By the way, your wife is a very lucky woman.
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about company being sold
jokes.punchline: Meredith's inappropriate personal comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Meredith inappropriate timing. Good character integration but predictable humor.

dialogue.speaker: Meredith
dialogue.line: Well, I guess this means no more free coffee in the break room. That's fine. I've been meaning to cut back anyway. My liver could use the break.

jokes.text: Nice to meet you, oscar. Nice to meet you, mark. It's matt. Right, matt. I know what I'm doing, pam.
jokes.setup: Pam's awkward matchmaking continues
jokes.punchline: Matt corrects his own name while Pam claims expertise
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Pam's confidence despite obvious failure. Name confusion adds to awkwardness.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: So Matt, Oscar tells me you're single and looking to mingle!

dialogue.speaker: Matt
dialogue.line: Actually, it's Mitt.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Right, Matt. Anyway, I'm basically a professional matchmaker at this point.

dialogue.speaker: Oscar
dialogue.line: You've never successfully set anyone up.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: That's not true. I have a system.

jokes.text: I can use this for so many nuts--macadamias, Brazil nuts, pecans, almonds. Clams. Snails.
jokes.setup: Creed excited about nutcracker gift
jokes.punchline: List escalates from nuts to inappropriate non-nuts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed escalation - starts normal then gets weird with clams and snails. Good structure and character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Oh wow, a nutcracker! Finally, something to crack open my walnuts, pecans, Brazil nuts, macadamia nuts, pine nuts, pistachios...

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: ...hazelnuts, cashews, almonds, chestnuts, coconuts, peanuts, acorns, hickory nuts...

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: ...and human skulls.

jokes.text: I would like an xbox and... A tv that's compatible with an xbox. And I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Michael sitting on Phyllis's Santa lap
jokes.punchline: Childish wish followed by genuine apology
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Michael's genuine childlike wish and rare sincere apology. Nice emotional turn.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I want a pony.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Michael, you're too old for a pony.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, you're right. I want a blowjob.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I apologize. That was inappropriate.

jokes.text: Twelve drummers drumming. Merry Christmas, Aaron.
jokes.setup: Final day of Andy's twelve days of Christmas gifts
jokes.punchline: Literal twelve drummers show up, Andy calls her wrong name
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to running gag with final escalation. Wrong name adds character comedy to the moment.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Erin, what is this?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Twelve drummers drumming! For the twelve days of Christmas!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Erin, you didn't. You actually hired twelve drummers?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: Of course I did! You loved all the other gifts!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: This is insane. There are twelve actual drummers in this office right now.

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: I know! Isn't it amazing?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, it's— wait, what's your name again?

dialogue.speaker: Erin
dialogue.line: It's Erin.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Right, right. Well, Irene, this was a mistake.

tmdb_overview: A banker representing a potential buyer comes to the Scranton branch to interview the H.R. department before the sale goes through.
insight: Weak joke execution drags down a solid 55-joke setup into mid-tier territory.
